overview: Rakdos Delirium functions as a tempo deck that applies immediate pressure with low-cost creatures while cycling through card types to trigger delirium. By leveraging the graveyard synergies of its signature one- and two-drops, the deck generates incremental damage and board presence before opponents can stabilize. The strategy relies on efficient sequencing and delirium-boosted value to outpace Modern's diverse meta.
win_conditions: Triggering delirium to unlock recursive value and board control
Overwhelming opponents with a wide board of cheap, resilient creatures
Accumulating incremental damage through death triggers and combat

keep_criteria: Hands containing at least two cheap creatures or delirium-enabling cards
Early interaction or recursion to stabilize against aggressive decks
Mana base that ensures smooth turns two and three
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing non-foils or duals if available), at least one delirium enabler (discard spell, graveyard filler, or Nethergoyf/Moonshadow), and a threat or interaction.
Prioritize hands that can hit turn 1 delirium or establish board presence by turn 2.
mulligan_criteria: Lack of early plays or delirium progress
Hands heavy on late-game cards without early board presence
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early plays, or 2+ lands with only threats and no enablers/interaction.

early_game: Play Nethergoyf and Moonshadow on curve to establish early board presence
Use cheap interaction to disrupt opponent's early game
Begin cycling cards to different types to progress toward delirium
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a discard spell or low-cost delirium enabler to start filling the graveyard.
If you have Nethergoyf or Moonshadow, play it on turn 1 or 2 once delirium is active.
Turn 2: Drop a second land, cast another enabler or threat.
If delirium is active, prioritize deploying scalable creatures.
Land sequencing: Play non-basic lands first if they enter untapped, then basics to avoid summoning sickness on key turns.
mid_game: Deploy Cori-Steel Cutter and other threats to apply consistent pressure
Trigger delirium to unlock recursive value and board control spells
Maintain board advantage by trading efficiently and leveraging graveyard synergies
Preserve mana for delirium triggers and threat deployment.
Use graveyard fillers efficiently—don't dump everything if you're behind on board.
Prioritize keeping hand size above 3 to maintain disruption.
Commit threats when you have delirium active and opponent has no immediate removal.
Hold back if they have sweepers or spot removal.
late_game: Use delirium-boosted recursion to repeatedly deploy signature creatures
Stabilize with wide boards and card advantage engines
Close out games with overwhelming board presence or targeted finishers
Nethergoyf (massively scaled), Moonshadow, and Cori-Steel Cutter once delirium is stable.
Win by trading efficiently and deploying a delirium-powered threat they can't answer.
When delirium is active, you have board presence, and opponent has no sweepers or spot removal.
Go all-in if you can close the game in 1-2 turns.

key_cards.name: Moonshadow
key_cards.role: Early aggressive threat with flash and deathtouch
key_cards.notes: Play on opponent's turn to dodge removal or set up combat tricks; use deathtouch to trade up efficiently and protect larger threats.

key_cards.name: Nethergoyf
key_cards.role: One-drop creature that deals damage upon death
key_cards.notes: Cycle early to trigger death triggers, progress delirium, and apply incremental pressure; sacrifice or trade it when it offers the best value.

key_cards.name: Cori-Steel Cutter
key_cards.role: Two-drop threat with a death trigger
key_cards.notes: Deploy on curve to maintain board presence; use its death trigger to chip away at opponent's life total or trigger additional graveyard effects.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize playing creatures that progress delirium before casting expensive spells
Hold up flash creatures like Moonshadow to respond to opponent's removal or combat
Cycle low-value cards early to reach four card types in the graveyard faster
Save removal for opponent's key threats rather than trading early creatures unnecessarily
avoid overfilling the graveyard with useless cards if you're facing graveyard hate.
Track opponent's removal and sweepers carefully—overextending with delirium threats is the most common mistake.
Moonshadow and Nethergoyf scale differently, so prioritize which one to commit based on board state.
Cori-Steel Cutter likely synergizes with delirium triggers, so time its activation for maximum value.

matchups.advice: Play aggressively to race while using cheap removal to clear the path; prioritize delirium progress to unlock value.
Apply steady pressure with cheap creatures to force early interaction; use delirium recursion to outvalue their hand traps and board clears.
Race before they stabilize with larger threats; leverage graveyard synergies to recover from trades and maintain board presence.
Use discard to remove counterspells from hand. Cast threats in response to their spells. Use multiple low-cost threats to force counter trade-offs.
Apply early pressure. Use discard to hit their ramp spells. Don't let them stabilize; trade efficiently and win before they cast their finisher.
Win the discard war. Prioritize targeting their enablers and threats. Use targeted removal efficiently. Control the graveyard by dumping key types when advantageous.

common_threats: Life total pressure from aggressive strategies
Watch for artifact acceleration (Affinity/Amulet), combo pieces (Ruby Storm), or blink/evade threats (Jeskai).
Note when they tap out for sweepers or counterspells.
Track graveyard contents if they have hate.
